text.title: Django Service Layer Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying django-services. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: services/orders.py imports HttpRequest to read user.id from a request inside place_order().
antipattern.root-cause: Author shortcut the refactor by passing the whole request through.
antipattern.fix: Refactor: pass user_id primitive into the service. Add import-linter contract: services/ may not import django.http.*.

antipattern.symptom: Views still call Model.objects.create(...) for some 'small' operations after partial refactor.
antipattern.root-cause: Partial migration; some 'simple' operations never moved to services.
antipattern.fix: Sweep with a codemod that finds Model.objects.(create|save|delete) outside services/. Move each call through a service function.

antipattern.symptom: Services organised as services/views.py, services/admin.py, services/tasks.py — same aggregate logic duplicated.
antipattern.root-cause: Module structure mirrors callers instead of aggregates.
antipattern.fix: Reorganise by aggregate: services/orders.py, services/payments.py. Each function called from any number of caller types.

antipattern.symptom: All tests use Django test client; service-layer not tested directly.
antipattern.root-cause: Habit from fat-view era; test client covers everything but slowly.
antipattern.fix: Add direct pytest tests against service functions with pydantic inputs. Keep client tests only for HTTP-layer concerns (auth, content type).
